title_of_original: Judgment of the Court (First Chamber) of 9 November 2017 - European Commission v TV2/Danmark A/S.
decided_by: Court of Justice of the European Union
case_description: [1] LatTeleGrid SIA (hereinafter LatTeleGrid) is a Latvian public television broadcaster established in 2003 as an autonomous State undertaking. [2] It is Latvia’s second-largest national broadcaster, [3] alongside Latvijas Valsts Radio un Televīzijas Centrs (LVRTC). [4]LatTeleGrid’s mission, as defined in a ministerial decree issued under the Broadcasting Act, [5] is to ensure the nationwide availability of public service television content. [6] This includes educational, cultural, and informational programming accessible free of charge to the public via terrestrial and digital platforms. [7] The specific content categories and coverage targets are outlined in a multi-year service mandate approved by the Latvian Minister for Culture. [8] LatTeleGrid was designed to operate under a mixed funding model. [9] At the outset, it received an interest-bearing State loan and a portion of broadcasting license fees paid by households through regulated tariffs. [10] In addition, the Latvian legislature introduced a mechanism enabling LatTeleGrid to benefit from advertising revenue generated during its programming. [11] Under this scheme, LatTeleGrid’s broadcasting time included scheduled slots for commercial advertising, sponsorships, and promotional messages. [12] From 2017 to 2021, LatTeleGrid did not manage its advertising sales directly. [13]Instead, this function was carried out by BāzesTīkls SIA, [14] a separate State-owned public undertaking [15] whose sole shareholder was the Republic of Latvia. [16] BāzesTīkls SIA marketed and sold advertising slots during LatTeleGrid’s programming, [17] collected the resulting revenue, [18] and transferred the proceeds to a public entity known as the LatTeleGrid Fund. [19] This Fund had been created under secondary legislation by the Minister for Culture to collect, manage, and allocate revenues intended to support LatTeleGrid’s public service mission. [20] The applicable law granted the Minister for Culture discretion over the use of the Fund’s resources, [21] including the authority to allocate advertising revenue to LatTeleGrid, [22] to redirect it to other media-related cultural initiatives, [23] or to retain it for future distribution. [24] In practice, the Minister approved full transfers of the advertising revenue for the years 2017 to 2021 to LatTeleGrid. [25] These transfers far exceeded the cost LatTeleGrid had to bear for its services.
gold_parsing: compensation_for_obligation('transfer of advertising revenue', 'LatTeleGrid', 'public tv broadcasting'). %[10]
adopted_by('transfer of advertising revenue', 'minister for culture'). %[24]
type_of_measure: Transfer of Advertising and Digital Service Revenue
